position_summary: Claude's revision is now technically aligned with the main risks: the repo-facing P0s remain non-negotiable, and the video plan correctly treats caveats as labels rather than apologies. The new risk is practical: accepting every Tier-1 item would spend scarce seconds on credibility reinforcement after credibility is already established, instead of maximizing early comprehension under the 3:00 cap.

findings.title: Minimal Cold-Open Is Highest ROI If It Replaces, Not Adds
findings.evidence: The current unique differentiator appears at frame 7 around 1:44. Claude proposes adding a 5-8s IL2 cold-open to an already 2:52 cut, leaving only 0-3s of headroom unless other material is removed.
findings.concern_path: If the IL2 refusal is simply inserted, the cut blows past 3:00 or forces rushed captions later. If it replaces part of the title/setup, judges see the distinctive referee behavior inside the first 10 seconds while the video remains clean.
findings.suggested_action: Must-do, but implement as a swap: open with 5-6s of IL2 -> UNTESTED, then a shorter title card. Recover time by cutting the title narration from 10s to about 5s and trimming one sentence across frames 2-4.

findings.title: Frame-8 Scope Fix Is Mandatory And Cheap
findings.evidence: Frame 8 currently combines 'NAB2 -> Th1/Th2 polarization -> atopic eczema' with 'referee verdict: supported', while the later STAT6 caption clarifies that the disease label remains a nomination.
findings.concern_path: A judge may remember the payoff slide as claiming support for the full disease causal chain. This would undercut the project's central calibration claim and create a mismatch with the repo and reviewer beat.
findings.suggested_action: Must-do. Change the visible label to 'program hop supported' plus 'eczema nomination inherited' or equivalent. This costs essentially no runtime and protects the science claim.

findings.title: Use One Callout, Not Three, Unless Time Is Recovered Elsewhere
findings.evidence: Frames 5, 6, and 10 are all small live Claude Science screenshots. Claude proposes three callouts, but each callout requires dwell time or visual attention during an already dense 2:52 video.
findings.concern_path: Three callouts can turn the middle third into an audit UI tour. The judging gain is highest when one live-proof moment becomes readable enough to establish that the receipts are real; after that, overlays carry the story better than more screenshot parsing.
findings.suggested_action: Must-do only for frame 10 if choosing one: highlight STAT6 +0.09/adj_p 0.79 and NAB2 self -3.08 because it supports the confounder-refutation claim. If a second is affordable, call out frame 5's live HTTP counts. Cut the frame 6 callout and replace its equals-chain caption with plain wording only.

findings.title: Reviewer Beat Is Valuable But Should Be A Caption/Narration Tightening, Not A New Beat
findings.evidence: Frame 11 is already visually legible and the narration already says the reviewer model flagged 'validated' and 'definitive'. Claude proposes isolating it as its own beat with an added workbench line.
findings.concern_path: Making it a separate expanded beat competes with the NAB2 and STAT6 payoff. The Claude-use value is real, but the current frame already conveys it if the line is sharpened.
findings.suggested_action: Nice-to-have in minimal form: keep frame 11 duration roughly unchanged, revise the spoken line to 'Anthropic's scientific workbench checked the claim, flagged validated and definitive, and I cut them.' Do not add a separate scene unless time remains after the cold-open and scope fixes.

findings.title: Honesty Caption Rewrite Is Cheap And Should Replace The Dense Chain
findings.evidence: The current B5 caption says 'full 22,039 sweep = CS-native cached-receipt replay · live proof = micro-sweep · falsification = STAT6 S3', which is accurate but hard to parse.
findings.concern_path: The caption can read defensive at exactly the moment the video is trying to show live work. Rewriting it improves comprehension without adding runtime if it replaces the existing lower-third.
findings.suggested_action: Must-do if it is a text swap only. Use two short captions across existing shots: 'Live micro-sweep: generator plus receipt path.' and 'Full 22,039 sweep: cached replay, same deterministic referee.' Do not add new dwell solely for this.

findings.title: New Risk: The Revised Plan Overweights Meta-Credibility After The Science Is Already Credible
findings.evidence: Claude's accepted Tier-1 list includes cold-open, three callouts, split-label frame, isolated reviewer beat, confounder wording, two honesty captions, and an R01 sentence, all under a hard 3:00 cap from an existing 2:52 cut.
findings.concern_path: The final version could feel like a sequence of qualifications: live/cached, nomination, confounder, reviewer, R01 critique. That risks flattening the clean arc: question generator -> deterministic referee -> confident no -> NAB2 survivor -> confounder checked -> calibrated conclusion.
findings.suggested_action: Cut the R01 sentence from the video unless it replaces existing narration cleanly. Keep it in the written summary. Also do not add all three callouts or expand the reviewer beat; prioritize early IL2, frame-8 scoping, and one decisive STAT6 callout.

findings.title: New Risk: 'Thirty Survive' Still Needs Careful Scope In Spoken And Visual Text
findings.evidence: Frame 8 and the B5 narration say 'Thirty survive' after 22,039 hypotheses, while Claude's revised summary says to add the eczema=GWAS-nomination honesty line and lean on NAB2.
findings.concern_path: 'Thirty survive' can be heard as thirty disease-level discoveries unless scoped. The repo may be calibrated, but the video payoff could still overstate what survived: clean, receipt-backed candidates under the referee's hop criteria, not validated disease mechanisms.
findings.suggested_action: Revise the narration or caption to 'Thirty survive the referee gates' or 'Thirty clean candidates survive.' Pair that with the frame-8 split label so survival is not confused with disease causality.

escalated_findings.rationale: Accepted, but the implementation detail is unresolved: it must be a replacement/edit, not an insertion, because the cut is already 2:52 under a 3:00 cap.
Accepted, but three callouts are too expensive for the available runway. The minimal version should prioritize the STAT6 evidence callout, with frame 5 second and frame 6 dropped.
Accepted, but 'isolated beat' risks over-expansion. The value can be captured with a sharper existing line and unchanged visual dwell.
Claude reduced R01 to one sentence, but under the hard cap even that is lower ROI than early IL2, claim scoping, and a decisive live evidence callout.
Claude accepts the warning, but the revised Tier-1 list still contains enough additions to create the exact 'list of defenses' failure mode.

dropped_findings.rationale: Fully accepted as Tier 0 with the right concrete actions: README rewrite, banned-term purge, path scrub, label/license reconciliation, citations, and final secret/path grep.
Addressed when paired with F-115: de-number frame 8 while preserving receipt texture and artifact identity.
Addressed: changing 'hardest confounder' to 'one key confounder' fixes the overreach without weakening the story.
Substantively accepted; remaining concern is only implementation scope, handled in this round's ROI ranking.
Accepted, though still marked as a must-do because the final cut depends on executing this exact text change.
Accepted: the plan preserves receipt texture rather than making the payoff look like a decorative marketing slide.
Correctly retained as QA boundary: voice timbre, music mix, and actual caption dwell require a human listen-through and cannot be judged from frames.

recommended_next_question: debate concluded: implement Tier 0, then recut with only the high-ROI subset: IL2 cold-open as a swap, frame-8 scope fix, plain cached/live caption swap, one STAT6 callout, and a tightened reviewer line only if it does not add runtime.
